summary: Backend-only retest scoped to the PDF Turkish character fix (bundled DejaVuSans/DejaVuSans-Bold in /app/backend/fonts). Wrote pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_pdf_export_turkish.py (11 tests) covering: /api/ health, /api/export/pdf validity + magic bytes, presence of Turkish headings & column headers (Kayıt Listesi, Oluşturulma, Aylık Onay Adetleri, Danışman Performansı, Marka Bazlı Toplamlar, Detaylı Kayıt Listesi, Müşteri, ŞASİ, TRAFİK, Danışman), lower/upper case Turkish glyph coverage (ı ş ü ö İ Ş Ç Ğ Ü Ö), no fallback glyph ■/□, filter combos (durum=ONAY/RED, year, year+month, marka+danisman), and Excel non-regression (3 sheets: Kayitlar, Danisman Performansi, Marka Toplamlari). All 11 tests PASSED. PDF text extraction confirms Turkish names render intact: ORÇUN DEMİRAY, SEHER SÖNMEZ, OLCAY ÇEVİREN, MİRAÇ SADIK ŞAHİN, EBRU KOCABAŞ, HANDE GÖROĞLU BAYSAN, ÖMER FARUK GÜNEŞ, ÇUHADUR COŞKUN, BARIŞ İSMAİL NALCI. Fix verified.
critical_code_review_comments: Font registration loop at server.py:585 mutates module-level _PDF_FONT / _PDF_FONT_BOLD only inside try; if TTFont() raises for bundled file, the code silently keeps Helvetica. Acceptable, but log level is warning — good.
server.py is now ~803 lines; approaching the recommended 700-line split threshold. Consider splitting export handlers (excel/pdf) into a separate module in a future refactor.
PDF font candidate order correctly prefers bundled files over /usr/share/fonts — good, since OS fonts were missing (root cause of original bug).
seed_data_creation: None. DB already had 321 records from prior iteration; used as-is.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PDF Turkish char fix verified via pypdf text extraction. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_pdf_export_turkish.py can be re-run any time with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/test_pdf_export_turkish.py -v. Note: lowercase 'ç', 'ğ', 'ö' were relaxed to (lower OR upper) checks because none of the app's hardcoded strings or DB data contain those lowercase letters — uppercase Ç/Ğ/Ö all render correctly, proving font coverage. Fallback-glyph absence check (■/□) is the strongest anti-regression assertion.
rca of the issue: Root cause (already fixed by main agent): reportlab could not find DejaVuSans.ttf on the container's /usr/share/fonts path so it fell back to Helvetica, which lacks Turkish diacritic glyphs — output was replaced with ■/□. Mitigation: bundled DejaVuSans.ttf + DejaVuSans-Bold.ttf into /app/backend/fonts/ and added them as the first entries in _FONT_CANDIDATES (server.py:579-584), registered via pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont(...)). Verified: extracted PDF text contains all Turkish glyphs, none of ■/□, across default and filtered exports.
